ha.note: HA sirens expose on/off only; 'strobe' and 'both' are unreachable on read

event_type values are integration-specific (Z2M/ZHA/Hue differ); map covers common values, extend per integration
'tested' unreachable in HA
numeric ppm CO sensors exist; not the enum attribute, listed for picker awareness only
webCoRE color type; convert [r,g,b] to hex string
per-light min/max via min_color_temp_kelvin/max_color_temp_kelvin
only present when hvac_mode is heat_cool
fallback when hvac_mode is cool (single-setpoint mode)
only present when hvac_mode is heat_cool
fallback when hvac_mode is heat (single-setpoint mode)
same source as color
same source as button; held is just an event_type value
HA state attr is brightness 0-255; brightness_pct is write-only
HA transitional/jammed states collapse to 'unknown'; 'unlocked with timeout' unreachable in HA
no device_class; Z2M exposes *_linkquality sensors, ZHA exposes LQI diagnostic sensor - match by entity naming
HA has a ph device_class since 2023
no standard HA representation; some integrations expose an attribute
person state can also be a named zone; any zone name other than home maps to 'not present'
mmWave presence sensors; also matched by motion attribute - picker offers both
HA reports dBm (negative), webCoRE expects 0-100%; expose raw dBm and document mismatch rather than fake a conversion
'canceled' unreachable; HA timer has no canceled-vs-finished distinction in state
overlaps acceleration source; picker offers both
'tested' unreachable in HA
HA state set is wider than webCoRE playing/paused/stopped; buffering treated as playing, everything inactive as stopped
any non-off media_player state counts as on
HA fan_modes are integration-specific strings; values outside auto/on/circulate (low/medium/high etc.) pass through unmapped
HA heat_cool is webCoRE 'auto'; HA 'auto' (schedule-driven) also mapped to auto; dry/fan_only unmapped; 'emergency heat' unreachable in HA
pending heat/cool and vent economizer unreachable; off/preheating/defrosting/drying collapsed to nearest webCoRE value
single-setpoint modes only; heat_cool exposes high/low via cooling/heatingSetpoint
HA remaining is an HH:MM:SS string and is only a snapshot while paused; convert to integer seconds
webCoRE trackData is a JSON blob; closest HA equivalent is the media_* attribute set - revisit if a piston actually uses it
no uv_index device_class in HA; match by entity naming/unit 'UV index'
webCoRE valve is binary open/closed; HA transitional states collapse
many water valves are exposed as switches; picker decision whether to offer
'partially open' requires a position check: state open with current_position between 1-99; compiler/shim derives it
only some tracker integrations expose it
only some tracker integrations expose it
duration only where supported
collapses to siren.turn_on
notify implementation blocked pending phone on test HA per COMPILER_DECISIONS_HOLDING
only where the integration exposes circulate
alarm capability off command; cover.close does NOT belong here - that is the 'close' command
only locks with the OPEN feature (latch); picker should hide otherwise
webCoRE pause is shared by music player and timed session capabilities; route by domain
volume param {1} compiles as a preceding media_player.volume_set
same as playTextAndResume; HA does not distinguish resume vs restore
maps to HA announce feature (2024+) where the player supports it; otherwise degrade to playText
same as refresh in HA
webCoRE param is Kelvin, no conversion; clamp to light min/max kelvin
heat_cool mode requires both high and low in one call; single cool mode uses temperature: {0} - compiler branches on hvac_mode
mirror of setCoolingSetpoint
HA has no hue-only service; compile with template reading current hs_color[1]
param {1} (only-if-switch-is) compiles as a state condition, not service data
HA has no saturation-only service; compile with template reading current hs_color[0]
webCoRE 0-100 to HA 0.0-1.0
webCoRE auto -> HA heat_cool; emergency heat unmapped, hidden in picker
HA cannot adjust a running timer; restart with new duration
HA has no siren-vs-strobe distinction; tone param only where supported
targets the configured tts entity; see SPEAK_ACTION_SPEC. verify_ssl caveat applies on local HA certs
timedSession stop = finish now; timer.cancel is the 'cancel' command
collapses to siren.turn_on
same as setCoolingSetpoint semantics
generic fallback for any toggleable domain

setAdjustedHSLColor.d: Transition to color H:{0}° / S:{1}% / L:{2}% in {3}{4}

ha.ha: delay / wait statement in compiled script

p.d: and wait for execution to finish
p.w: webCoRE can only wait on piston executions of pistons within the same instance as the caller. Please note that global variables updated in the callee piston do NOT get reflected immediately in the caller piston, the new values will be available on the next run.

ha.ha: compiler variable store (piston local) or HA helper entity (global)

setTileColor.d: Set piston tile #{0} colors to {1} over {2}{3}

setTile.d: Set piston tile #{0} title  to "{1}", text to "{2}", footer to "{3}", and colors to {4} over {5}{6}

ha.ha: alarm_control_panel.alarm_arm_home / alarm_arm_away / alarm_arm_night / alarm_disarm on the designated HSM panel
ha.note: HSM maps to a designated alarm_control_panel per locked v2 decision

writeToFuelStream.d: Write data point '{2}' to fuel stream {0}{1}{3}

lifxBreathe.d: Breathe LIFX lights matching {0} to color {1}{2}{3}{4}{5}{6}{7}

p.d: with a peak at {v}% of the period

lifxPulse.d: Pulse LIFX lights matching {0} to color {1}{2}{3}{4}{5}{6}

is_greater_than_or_equal_to.dd: are greater than or equal to

was_less_than_or_equal_to.dd: were less than or equal to

was_greater_than_or_equal_to.d: was greater than or equal to
was_greater_than_or_equal_to.dd: were greater than or equal to

stays_less_than_or_equal_to.d: stays less than or equal to
stays_less_than_or_equal_to.dd: stay less than or equal to

stays_greater_than_or_equal_to.d: stays greater than or equal to
stays_greater_than_or_equal_to.dd: stay greater than or equal to

_ha_translation.purpose: HA read/write translation rules living alongside the locked webCoRE vocab. The webCoRE fields (n,t,o,r,u,p,d,i,a) are law and must never be edited; the 'ha' key is additive and editable. Absence of 'ha' on a key means not yet mapped; 'ha': 'n/a' means confirmed no HA equivalent (never offered in picker/wizard).
_ha_translation.attribute_rule: domain, optional device_class list, read ('state' or 'attr:<name>'), optional map (HA value -> webCoRE value; '*' = any other value), optional scale (expression on raw value), tag (verified|assumed|decision), optional note. Rule order = match priority. domain '*' = any domain.
_ha_translation.command_rule: domain, service, optional data ({0},{1}.. reference webCoRE command params in p-array order; {target} = configured target entity), tag, optional note. Rule order = domain match priority.
_ha_translation.consumers: shim device-payload builder uses attribute read rules to decide which capabilities an entity advertises; compiler uses command write rules for service routing. One file, both directions.
_ha_translation.pending: webCoRE places family (places, currentPlace, previousPlace, closestPlace, closestPlaceDistance, arrivingAtPlace, leavingAtPlace, distance, eta) intentionally unmapped: candidate future mapping is HA zones + proximity integration, decision deferred
